author: fabrizio
text: 🚨🔵 EXCLUSIVE: Chelsea have opened talks with AC Milan for Mike Maignan. Personal terms will not be an issue — the goalkeeper is keen on a Premier League move. Milan want €35m, negotiations ongoing. 🇫🇷 #CFC

author: danny
text: A WORLD CLASS KEEPER. IN JANUARY. WHILE WE'RE 4TH AND IN A CUP SEMI. THE REBUILD IS NOT A MEME ANYMORE PEOPLE 😭💙

quote.author: fabrizio
quote.text: 🚨🔵 EXCLUSIVE: Chelsea have opened talks with AC Milan for Mike Maignan. Personal terms will not be an issue…

author: chelseafc
text:
Wesley Fofana has undergone a scan on the hamstring injury sustained in Sunday's win over Tottenham and will be assessed over the coming weeks.

Everyone at Chelsea wishes Wes a speedy recovery. 💙

thread.author: sue
thread.text: "Assessed over the coming weeks" is Chelsea for "see you in March". I'm not even angry anymore, I'm just tired. Wrap Colwill in bubble wrap immediately.

author: ornstein
text: 🚨 EXCLUSIVE: Chelsea in advanced talks to extend Cole Palmer's contract to 2031 on significantly improved terms. Club view 23yo as cornerstone of project under Joaquín Rea + hierarchy confident of agreement before January window @TheAthleticFC

author: terry
text: Maignan talks, Palmer contract, cup semi. Look, credit where it's due — Rea has finally listened about the keeper. I've only been saying it since AUGUST. In my day the board would've taken 3 years to work that out. #CFC

author: fabrizio
text: Understand Chelsea are also monitoring the centre-back market after Wesley Fofana's injury — loan deal with option preferred. Three names on the list, all with Premier League experience. More soon. 🔵 #CFC

author: marcus
text:
Chelsea 5-1 Spurs by the numbers:

• xG: 3.8 – 0.6
• 14 shots inside the box
• Estêvão: 9 completed take-ons (most by a CFC player in a PL game this season)
• PPDA: 8.2, best pressing display of the Rea era

That wasn't a derby, that was a lecture. 🧵👇

author: terry
text: Great result, won't hear a word against the lads. BUT. It should have been seven. Delap has to bury that chance in the 80th. Standards, that's all I'm saying. Fergie would've hairdryered him at 5-1. #CFC

author: sue
text:
I have now watched Estêvão's second goal 14 times and I still don't understand how he got out of that corner. We might actually have one here.

(We'll sell him to Madrid in 2027, but still.)

author: palmer
text: Cold night. Big win. This group is special 🥶💙

author: enzo
text: VAMOS!! 💙 Gran victoria en casa con nuestra gente. @Estevao qué jugador 🤩 #CFC

author: joaopedro
text: Blessed 🙏 3 goals in my last 4. This is what we work for every day. Obrigado Bridge, you were LOUD tonight 💙

author: gaz
text: Chelsea fans acting like they've won the league because they beat 10-man Spurs 😂😂 It's DECEMBER. See you in May when you're scrapping for 4th as usual. Saved this tweet lads.

thread.author: chloe
thread.text: Gaz babes, we knocked you out of the Carabao two weeks ago and you blocked me for three days. Log off x

author: optajoe
text: 3 - Estêvão (18y 229d) is the youngest Chelsea player to be directly involved in 3+ goals in a Premier League London derby (2 goals, 1 assist vs Tottenham). Joia. 💎

author: skysports
text:
Chelsea put FIVE past Tottenham at the Bridge 🤯

Who was your man of the match? #CHETOT

author: danny
text: 5-1. FIVE. ONE. AGAINST THAT LOT. ESTÊVÃO IS HIM. PALMER IS HIM. JOÃO PEDRO IS HIM. I'M NOT DOING THE "LET'S STAY CALM" THING, WE ARE SO BACK 😭💙💙 KTBFFH

author: chelseafc
text:
FULL-TIME: Chelsea 5-1 Tottenham 🔵

Palmer ⚽⚽
Estêvão ⚽⚽
João Pedro ⚽

WHAT. A. DERBY. 💙 #CHETOT

author: skysports
text:
FT: Chelsea 5-1 Tottenham.

An absolute demolition at Stamford Bridge. Palmer (2), Estêvão (2) and João Pedro run riot after Romero's first-half red card. Joaquín Rea's rebuild is starting to look very, very real. #CHETOT

author: skysports
text:
🟥 RED CARD! Cristian Romero is sent off for hauling down Estêvão, who was clean through after skinning him on the halfway line.

Chelsea 2-0 Tottenham (38') #CHETOT

author: chelseafc
text:
TEAM NEWS 📋 Your Blues to face Tottenham!

Sánchez; James (c), Chalobah, Colwill, Cucurella; Caicedo, Fernández; Estêvão, Palmer, Neto; João Pedro.

Subs: Jörgensen, Fofana, Acheampong, Lavia, Santos, Paz, Gittens, Delap, Guiu. #CHETOT

author: chloe
text: Right. Derby eve. Be honest with yourselves for once — where do we ACTUALLY finish this season? 💙

author: optajoe
text: 7 - Nico Paz has created 7 big chances since the start of October, more than any other Chelsea midfielder in that period. Settling. 🎨

author: terry
text: £52m for Nico Paz and he's started what, half our league games? Lovely player, not doubting it, but in my day if you paid that money the lad PLAYED. Explain that one to me Rea.

thread.author: marcus
thread.text: Terry he was injured for six weeks. We've been over this. Twice.

thread.author: terry
thread.text: Injured. In my day you ran it off.

author: chelseafc
text:
Our Carabao Cup semi-final opponents: Newcastle United 🏆

First leg at St James' Park in early January, second leg at the Bridge. #CarabaoCup

author: fabrizio
text: Despite noise after Arsenal defeat, Chelsea board remain 100% behind Joaquín Rea — his position was never in discussion, project continues with full backing. Focus now on January window plans. 🔵 #CFC

author: terry
text: Since apparently I'm "overreacting" (Marcus). Fine. Let the people decide:

author: marcus
text:
Yes, it was 3-0. The xG was 1.9 – 1.4 and we were the better side for an hour before the second goal. I'm not saying it was fine. I'm saying it wasn't a crisis.

Deleting the replies telling me to touch grass, thank you.

author: sue
text: That was the most Chelsea performance of all time. Eight unbeaten. EIGHT. Lulled me into genuine belief, let me tell my Arsenal-supporting brother-in-law we were "different this year", and then produced THAT. I did this to myself.

author: terry
text: #ReaOut trended for two hours tonight and honestly, after that first half, can you blame anyone? You cannot go to the Emirates with ONE holding midfielder. Basics. Absolute basics. Mourinho would never.

author: skysports
text:
FT: Arsenal 3-0 Chelsea.

A harsh lesson at the Emirates for Joaquín Rea's young side, who had won five of their last six. Chelsea's eight-game unbeaten run ends in painful fashion. #ARSCHE
